    Fly Higher
    Concorde Tour
    Let your imagination soar during this 20-minute tour aboard this technological marvel!
    Learn More
    • Permanent Exhibitions
    • Temporary Exhibitions
    • Past Exhibitions
    • Go to Exhibitions Page
    Image
    Final Flight: The Story of a WWII Corsair
    On March 18, 1945, a young, newly married naval aviator flew from the USS Intrepid on his first combat mission; it would be his last. He and his F4U-1D Corsair were shot down near the city of Saiki on Japan’s southern main island of Kyushu.
    Learn More

    • Host an Event
    • Go to Events Page
    Image
    Astro Live and Astronomy Night Events
    Viewers who have a passion for space science participate and meet astronauts, scientists, engineers, and other talents through these monthly virtual and onsite programs that explore relevant space-themed topics.
    Learn More

    Image
    Give More Good
    Member Benefits
    From free admission year-round and special discounts to exclusive event invitations, being an Intrepid Museum member has a long list of benefits, so you can get more out of every visit.
    Learn More
    • What's New
    • Education
    • Accessibility Tools
    • View Our Collections
    • Go to Resources Page
    Image
    Your Education Destination
    Learning Library
    The Intrepid Museum Learning Library offers thematic Museum content and lessons to learners of all ages and abilities. Historic photographs, oral histories and videos enrich educational lessons and resources covering different subjects.
    Learn More
Museum Hours

Our hours are subject to change on a daily basis based on special events, capacity and exhibition closures. Please check our Visitor Information page.

Mon–Fri: 10:00am–5:00pm 
Sat–Sun & holidays: 10:00am–6:00pm
Last entry is one hour prior to closing.

The Museum is closed on Thanksgiving and Christmas Day.

Thursday, June 11 – Sunday, July 19 | Free and open to the public
Free World Cup Watch Parties at Pier 86
Join the Intrepid Museum at Pier 86 for free outdoor World Cup Watch Parties, sponsored by Bank of America, with stunning NYC skyline and Hudson River views.

Breadcrumb
Home Events Calendar

Calendar

Browse our event calendar to find events for all ages that let you learn and explore year-round.

Events Overview

From movie nights to behind-the-scenes looks at our most popular artifacts, events at the Intrepid Museum offer something for everyone.

Theme
Audience
Price
Location
Viewing 12 out of 42.
Image
Early Morning Opening: Soaring Science: Forces behind Flight
Jul 25, 2026 | 8:30am - 11am EDT

How does that fly?! Learn the science behind getting airplanes and helicopters up in the air through the Intrepid Museum’s collection of historic aircraft. Then, try hands-on art and science projects that test the forces of flight!

Learn More
Image
Virtual Astro Live: 7/26
Jul 26, 2026 | 3pm - 4:30pm EDT

Viewers who have a passion for space science participate and meet astronauts, scientists, engineers, and other talents through these monthly virtual and onsite programs that explore relevant space-themed topics.

Learn More
Image
Intrepid After Hours: Photography Workshop
Jul 29, 2026 | 4:30pm - 8pm EDT

Join fellow current and former service members for a photography workshop on the flight deck, led by veteran teaching artists from the Josephine Herrick Project.

Learn More
Image
Movie Night - National Treasure
Jul 31, 2026 | 7pm - 10pm EDT

This summer, the Intrepid Museum marks the 250th anniversary of the United States with a special edition of its annual Summer Movie Series.

Learn More
Image
Access Family Program: Sail-A-Bration! (Children)
Aug 09, 2026 | 11am - 1pm EDT

Discover how crew members aboard Intrepid celebrated special occasions with their shipmates while far from home. This program highlights U.S. Navy traditions and invites families to share their own meaningful traditions!

Learn More
Image
Access Family Program: Sail-A-Bration! (Teens and Adults)
Aug 09, 2026 | 2pm - 4pm EDT

How do you build the future of flight? By learning from the past!

Learn More
  • Load More
Our Programs

We offer a number of different programs and events that celebrate history, science, technology and engineering in fun and exciting ways.
